## ✅ Sprint 1 — Requirements & Design Foundation

**Sprint Duration:** 2 Weeks
**Start Date:** 2026-06-25
**End Date:** 2026-07-09
**Total Story Points:** 27 SP

### 🎯 Sprint Goal

> Establish the complete requirements baseline and system design foundation for the Food Delivery Platform, unblocking all development work in Sprint 2.

---

| # | Issue | Title | ADLC Phase | Priority | Story Points | Dependencies |
|---|-------|-------|------------|----------|:---:|---|

| 2 | #2 | Define Requirements for Restaurant Browsing and Discovery | Requirement Analysis | 🔴 High | 3 SP | None |
| 3 | #3 | Define Requirements for Cart, Payment, Order Tracking & Cancellation | Requirement Analysis | 🔴 High | 5 SP | None |
| 4 | #4 | Design System Architecture and Database Schema | Design | 🔴 High | 8 SP | #1, #2, #3 |

### 🔗 Dependency Map

```
#1 (Requirements: Auth)          ──┐
#2 (Requirements: Restaurant)    ──┼──► #4 (Architecture & DB Schema) ──► #5 (UX/UI Wireframes)
#3 (Requirements: Cart/Payment)  ──┘
```

> ⚠️ **#4 is the critical path blocker** — it depends on all 3 requirement issues and gates all downstream development sprints.

---

### 🗓️ Sprint 1 Execution Timeline

#### Week 1 — Parallel Requirement Analysis

> Issues #1, #2, and #3 have **no dependencies** and are executed **simultaneously**.

| Day | Activity | Issue(s) | Agent |
|-----|----------|----------|-------|
| Day 1–4 | Document auth, browsing, and cart/payment requirements in parallel | #1, #2, #3 | Requirements Analyst Agent |
| Day 5 | Stakeholder sign-off on all requirement documents | #1, #2, #3 | Requirements Analyst Agent |

#### Week 2 — Sequential Design

> Design work proceeds in strict dependency order.

| Day | Activity | Issue | Agent |
|-----|----------|-------|-------|
| Day 6–9 | Design system architecture, service boundaries, DB schema & API contracts | #4 | Solution Design Agent |
| Day 10–14 | Create Figma wireframes, UX flows & design system tokens for all screens | #5 | Solution Design Agent |

---

### 📌 Acceptance Criteria Summary

#### #1 — Define Requirements for User Registration and Login
- [ ] All required user data fields are documented (name, email, phone, password).
- [ ] Auth methods (email/password, OAuth) are specified.
- [ ] Password policy and validation rules are defined.
- [ ] Session duration and token strategy are documented.
- [ ] Edge cases (duplicate email, account lockout) are captured.
- [ ] Stakeholder sign-off obtained on scope.

#### #2 — Define Requirements for Restaurant Browsing and Discovery
- [ ] Search and filter parameters are fully listed.
- [ ] Location/geo-sorting behavior is documented.
- [ ] Restaurant detail page content (name, rating, hours, menu) is specified.
- [ ] Menu item fields (name, description, image, price, availability) are defined.
- [ ] Pagination and sorting rules are documented.

#### #3 — Define Requirements for Cart, Payment, Order Tracking, and Cancellation
- [ ] Cart rules are defined (max items, item quantity limits, restaurant-switching behavior).
- [ ] Supported payment methods are listed (card, wallet, COD if any).
- [ ] Order status lifecycle is documented (placed → confirmed → preparing → out for delivery → delivered).
- [ ] Cancellation eligibility window and refund policy are defined.
- [ ] Notification touchpoints (email, push, SMS) are identified.

#### #4 — Design System Architecture and Database Schema
- [ ] High-level system architecture diagram is produced.
- [ ] Service boundaries and responsibilities are defined.
- [ ] Database ERD covering users, restaurants, menus, orders, payments is completed.
- [ ] Core REST API endpoints are listed with request/response shapes.
- [ ] Technology stack is finalized and documented.
- [ ] Non-functional requirements (scalability, latency, availability) are addressed.

#### #5 — Design UX Flows and UI Wireframes for All App Screens
- [ ] Figma wireframes/mockups delivered for all screens.
- [ ] User flow diagrams cover all key journeys end-to-end.
- [ ] Design system tokens (colors, fonts, spacing, icons) are defined.
- [ ] Component library structure is agreed upon.
- [ ] Designs reviewed and approved by stakeholders.

---

### ⚠️ Risks & Blockers

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|:------:|------------|
| Delayed stakeholder sign-off on requirements | 🔴 High | Schedule sign-off sessions in advance during Week 1 |
| Architecture decisions in #4 causing rework downstream | 🔴 High | Involve all team leads during architecture review |
| UX design (#5) requiring multiple revision cycles | 🟡 Medium | Limit to 2 feedback rounds max within the sprint |
| Requirements ambiguity in #3 (payment/cancellation rules) | 🟡 Medium | Clarify payment gateway (Stripe) scope on Day 1 |
